questions.q: To which goddess is the Dakshineswar Temple primarily dedicated?
questions.explanation: The Dakshineswar Temple is dedicated to the goddess Kali, worshipped there in the form of Bhavatarini.

questions.q: On the bank of which river does the Dakshineswar Kali Temple stand?
questions.explanation: The temple sits on the eastern bank of the Hooghly River, a distributary of the Ganga, near Kolkata.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is the Dakshineswar Temple located?
questions.explanation: Dakshineswar is in West Bengal, just north of the city of Kolkata.

questions.q: Who founded the Dakshineswar Kali Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple was founded by Rani Rashmoni, a wealthy and philanthropic widow of Kolkata.

questions.q: Which famous 19th-century saint served as a priest at the Dakshineswar Temple?
questions.explanation: Sri Ramakrishna Paramahamsa served as a priest of the Kali temple and lived much of his life there.

questions.q: In which century was the Dakshineswar Temple built?
questions.explanation: The temple complex was completed in the mid-19th century, opening in 1855.

questions.q: The temple complex is located near which major Indian metropolis?
questions.explanation: Dakshineswar lies just north of Kolkata, the capital of West Bengal.

questions.q: The Dakshineswar Temple is associated with which religion?
questions.explanation: It is a Hindu temple, a major center of Shakti (goddess) worship.

questions.q: The main temple is built in which broad regional architectural style?
questions.explanation: The main shrine is built in the traditional Bengali Navaratna (nine-spired) temple style.

questions.q: Sri Ramakrishna is best known as the guru of which influential disciple?
questions.explanation: Swami Vivekananda was the foremost disciple of Sri Ramakrishna at Dakshineswar.

questions.q: In which year was the Dakshineswar Temple consecrated and opened for worship?
questions.explanation: The temple was consecrated on 31 May 1855 after several years of construction.

questions.q: The principal Kali idol at Dakshineswar is worshipped under which specific name?
questions.explanation: The Kali idol is known as Bhavatarini, meaning 'she who liberates from the ocean of existence'.

questions.q: Rani Rashmoni belonged to which non-Brahmin Bengali community, a fact that initially complicated finding priests for her temple?
questions.explanation: Rani Rashmoni came from the Mahishya (Kaivarta) community, a non-Brahmin group; because she was not a Brahmin, orthodox priests at first hesitated to serve at her temple.

questions.q: How many smaller Shiva temples line the riverfront of the Dakshineswar complex?
questions.explanation: Twelve identical Shiva temples in the Aatchala Bengali style stand in a row facing the river.

questions.q: Besides the main Kali shrine and Shiva temples, the complex also has a temple dedicated to which deity?
questions.explanation: The courtyard includes a temple to Radha-Krishna, called the Vishnu or Radhakanta temple.

questions.q: According to tradition, what inspired Rani Rashmoni to build the temple?
options: A royal land grant from the British
A vision/dream of the goddess Kali before a pilgrimage to Kashi
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that the goddess Kali appeared to Rani Rashmoni in a dream, directing her to build a temple on the Ganga instead of going on her planned pilgrimage to Kashi (Varanasi).

questions.q: Who was Rani Rashmoni's son-in-law that managed the temple's affairs and became a great patron of Sri Ramakrishna?
questions.explanation: Mathuranath Biswas, known as Mathur Babu, was married to Rani Rashmoni's daughter and, as manager of the temple, was a great patron of Sri Ramakrishna.

questions.q: Sri Ramakrishna's wife and spiritual companion, who also lived at Dakshineswar, was known by what name?
questions.explanation: Sarada Devi, revered as the Holy Mother, was Sri Ramakrishna's wife and lived in the Nahabat at Dakshineswar.

questions.q: The main Bhavatarini idol stands upon a reclining figure of which deity?
questions.explanation: Following Kali iconography, the goddess stands on the chest of a reclining Lord Shiva, both placed on a silver lotus.

questions.q: Roughly how tall is the main Navaratna Kali temple at Dakshineswar?
questions.explanation: The main temple rises to roughly 30 metres (about 100 feet) on a high raised platform.

questions.q: Which prominent Brahmo Samaj leader visited Dakshineswar and helped spread Sri Ramakrishna's fame among Kolkata's educated classes?
questions.explanation: Keshab Chandra Sen, a leader of the Brahmo Samaj, met Sri Ramakrishna and publicized his teachings in the 1870s.

questions.q: What is the name of the room near the temple where Sri Ramakrishna lived and received visitors?
options: His room at the north-west corner of the courtyard
questions.explanation: Sri Ramakrishna lived in a room at the north-western corner of the temple courtyard, now a pilgrimage site.

questions.q: Because Rani Rashmoni was not a Brahmin, who agreed to serve as the temple's first head priest, resolving the caste objection?
options: An official of the British East India Company
questions.explanation: After scholars devised a way around the caste objection, Ramkumar Chattopadhyay, Sri Ramakrishna's elder brother, agreed to serve and became the temple's first head priest in 1855.

questions.q: Sri Ramakrishna's birth name, before he became known by his monastic title, was what?
questions.explanation: Sri Ramakrishna was born Gadadhar Chattopadhyay in 1836 in Kamarpukur, Bengal.

questions.q: The grove of trees at Dakshineswar where Sri Ramakrishna practiced intense meditation is known as the?
questions.explanation: The Panchavati, a grove of five sacred trees planted near the temple, was where Sri Ramakrishna performed many of his spiritual disciplines.

questions.q: Approximately how large is the entire Dakshineswar temple compound?
questions.explanation: Rani Rashmoni acquired about 20 acres of land and the walled temple complex spans roughly 20 to 25 acres, including the courtyard, riverside ghats and subsidiary shrines.

questions.q: Rani Rashmoni had to overcome a major practical obstacle to import the deity and labour; what was she famous for opposing against the British?
options: A tax on fishing rights in the river / oppressive levies on the poor
questions.explanation: Rani Rashmoni was known for protecting poor fishermen, famously opposing British attempts to impose taxes/restrictions on fishing in the Hooghly.

questions.q: After Sri Ramakrishna's death in 1886, his disciples founded which organization that carries his name worldwide?
questions.explanation: Swami Vivekananda founded the Ramakrishna Mission in 1897 to continue his master's spiritual and humanitarian work.

questions.q: Which architectural feature gives the Navaratna ('nine-jewel') style its name at the main Kali temple?
options: Nine spires/towers (one central and eight surrounding)
questions.explanation: Navaratna refers to the nine spires, a large central shikhara surrounded by eight smaller towers on the upper storeys.

questions.q: Roughly how long did construction of the Dakshineswar temple complex take before its 1855 opening?
questions.explanation: Construction began around 1847 and the temple was consecrated in 1855, taking roughly eight years.
